name: Center for Fair Housing
description_short: Non-profit fair housing organization providing free advocacy, enforcement, housing counseling, and discrimination investigations in South Alabama since 2002.
description_long:
The Center for Fair Housing is a private, non-profit community resource based in Mobile, Alabama that has operated for 20 years with a mission to eliminate housing discrimination and promote housing choice. The organization is dedicated to serving communities affected by unfair housing practices and works to ensure equal opportunity regardless of protected class status. Founded with HUD support (FHIP Grant), the organization provides comprehensive fair housing services to South Alabama residents at no cost.

The Center for Fair Housing offers multiple free services including housing discrimination enforcement and investigation, mystery shopping investigations to identify discriminatory practices, outreach and education programs for the general public, specialized protections and services for persons with disabilities, housing counseling to guide individuals through homeownership steps, and volunteer opportunities for community members. They accept discrimination complaints via dedicated email (complaints@sacfh.com) and provide phone-based support during business hours. Their approach combines direct consumer advocacy with institutional compliance work targeting landlords, banks, and other housing-related organizations.

What distinguishes this organization is their focus on investigative enforcement and mystery shopping as tools to uncover systemic discrimination, combined with education and disability-specific protections. They operate with both individual complaint resolution and broader community education mandates. The organization explicitly names protected classes under fair housing law (race, color, national origin, religion, sex, disability, familial status, military status, and sexual orientation) and frames their work as fighting for affected individuals.

The primary limitation is geographic scope—services are specific to South Alabama from their Mobile, AL location. Operating hours are standard business hours (Monday-Friday, 8 AM-5 PM) with remote operations noted as COVID-related. The organization does not appear to offer credit-related services or financial counseling beyond housing-specific guidance. For individuals outside South Alabama or requiring credit repair rather than fair housing advocacy, this would not be the appropriate resource.
pros: Completely free services—no fees for discrimination complaints, counseling, or advocacy
HUD-funded organization with official fair housing enforcement authority and grants
20-year track record of community service with established credibility
Multiple avenues for complaints (phone, email, in-person) and free housing counseling
Mystery shopping investigations to identify discriminatory practices at properties
Specialized protections and services designed for persons with disabilities
Education programs and community outreach to prevent discrimination before it occurs
cons: Limited to South Alabama geographic area—services not available nationally
Remote operations model (as of website content date) may limit in-person access
Standard business hours only (Monday-Friday, 8 AM-5 PM); closed weekends
No apparent credit repair or credit counseling services offered
Services are fair housing-specific; cannot assist with non-housing discrimination issues
best_for: South Alabama residents who have experienced housing discrimination based on protected class
Individuals with disabilities seeking housing rights advocacy and specialized protections
Renters and homebuyers facing discrimination in rental, sale, financing, or insurance decisions
Community members seeking education on fair housing rights and protections
diagnosis: Best for South Alabama residents experiencing housing discrimination who need free legal advocacy and investigation support. Primary limitation is strict geographic scope (Mobile, AL area only) and fair housing focus—this organization does not assist with credit issues, general financial counseling, or discrimination outside the housing sector.
services: Housing discrimination complaint intake and investigation
Fair housing enforcement advocacy and case representation
Mystery shopping investigations of properties and lenders
Free housing counseling for homeownership preparation
Disability-specific fair housing protections and guidance
Fair Housing Act compliance oversight of landlords and banks
Protected class discrimination documentation and support
Fair housing resource distribution and information
typical_results_timeline: Counseling available within 1-2 weeks of contact.
